Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
William Mangin papers
Mangin, William
8413
7 cubic feet. 7 cubic feet.
1951-1990. Photographs, research material Files, publications, clippings, field notes and field notebooks, photographs, and slides of Vicos and the Barriadas. Collection material in English 7963479
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
September 2013
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, September 2013
Date modified:
Kristen Reichenbach, August 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
William Mangin acted as Field Director of the Vicos experimental station from 1952-1953. He also was involved in the Barriadas in Lima and the Peace Corps in Peru and served as professor of anthropology at Syracuse University. His research interests included squatter settlements and mental health issues in Peru.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Files, publications, clippings, field notes and field notebooks, photographs, and slides of Vicos and the Barriadas.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Miscellaneous Files and Documents
Series II. Mental Health Documents
Series III. Photographs
Series IV. Slides
Series V. Audio
Cite As:
William Mangin Papers, #8413. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Slides (photographs). Photographs. Field notes Vicos (Peru) Lima (Peru) -- Social conditions. Mental health -- Peru. Squatter settlements -- Peru -- Lima. Urbanization -- Peru -- Lima. Anthropology -- Research. Cornell Peru Project
